comment: Synthetic OM extracted JSON for Kimpton Angler Hotel — what the OM extraction agent would produce after parsing the full Offering Memorandum PDF. All currency in USD.

broker.process: Marketed sale, no call for offers date

broker_proforma.comment: Broker proforma assumes year-1 stabilized after PIP completion; 80 percent occupancy versus market 76.2 percent and TTM actual 76.2 percent — flagged as optimistic

renovation_pip.pip_scope: Soft-good refresh of guestrooms (2026), public area FF&E refresh, rooftop pool deck resurfacing, mechanical upgrade

in_place_debt.assumability: Subject to lender approval, 1.0% assumption fee

title_and_zoning.zoning: RM-3 Multiple Family Residential High Intensity
title_and_zoning.historic_designation: Architecturally significant - South Beach Local Historic District

raw_text_by_page.1:
OFFERING MEMORANDUM
Kimpton Angler Hotel
660 Washington Avenue
Miami Beach, FL 33139

A 132-key Lifestyle Boutique Hotel
South Beach Submarket

Marketed Exclusively by JLL Hotels & Hospitality Group
Gilda Perez-Alvarado, Senior Managing Director

Asking: $36,400,000 ($275,758 per key)
raw_text_by_page.12:
PROPERTY SUMMARY
The Kimpton Angler is a 132-key, 5-story lifestyle boutique hotel located at 660 Washington Avenue in the heart of South Beach, Miami Beach, FL. Built in 2015 and most recently renovated in 2019, the property features 142,000 square feet of gross building area, a rooftop pool, two food and beverage outlets (The Coast restaurant on the rooftop and Minnow Bar in the lobby), 4,200 square feet of meeting space, and 88 on-site valet parking spaces. The asset operates as a Kimpton Hotels & Restaurants franchise (IHG Hotels & Resorts). Pet-friendly with full beach service and complimentary bicycle program.

raw_text_by_page.61:
PIP REQUIREMENTS
IHG Brand letter dated February 15, 2026 estimates $5,280,000 ($40,000 per key) of required property improvement plan work over the next 9 months. Scope includes guestroom soft-good refresh, public area FF&E refresh, rooftop pool deck resurfacing, and mechanical equipment upgrade.
raw_text_by_page.72:
MARKET OVERVIEW
South Beach submarket TTM RevPAR of $238.09 reflects a Miami Beach market-leading occupancy of 76.2% and ADR of $312.45. Supply growth of 1.2% versus demand growth of 4.8% supports continued RevPAR expansion. Subject property indexes at 112.2 RGI, 110.3 ARI, and 101.7 MPI versus comp set.
